failure_movement.justification: Prediction uses iter002 as baseline (not iter003), making absolute starting counts wrong. Direction is correct for all three clusters: empty correctly predicted <8 (actual 7); wrong correctly predicted 7-10 (actual 10); unknown direction correct but magnitude underestimated (predicted ~29->20-24, actual 52->34). Partial credit for correct directions, penalized for wrong baseline.

trace_movement.justification: Prompt token drop to ~1500-1700 is directionally right (actual 1408.7, slightly below range). Completion tokens prediction (~1800 rise) is flatly wrong—actual dropped from 176.8 to 162.9. Tier-distribution claim (archival~70, recall~30, core~0) is contradicted by data: iter003 and iter004 both show recall~54%, archival~33%, identical to iter002. Two of three trace claims failed.

side_effects.justification: Correctly flagged wrong-answer regression risk (wrong cluster grew 9->10). Correctly noted token consumption lower than iter003. The reasoning_content verbose-non-answer risk is plausible but unverifiable. Missed: completion tokens actually fell (not rose), and tier distribution did not change, suggesting the scaffold diff was less impactful than predicted.
